Macro:

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
1) i don't think US will be interested to pay more yields for debt holders, but they will press the US for that.
I think yields will go above 5% in the next 1 year... but slowly.
the thing that now it is capped is most likely this "salesman thing"... they need buyers to bring in.
but after it cannot be surpressed for ever. Inflation will go up, and it will manifest in the datas.
when debt burden is softened a it, thats when the cycle starts.

2) no.

3) i like to get implied vol from VIX by myself. just like one does it with SPX. And it differs from VVIX approximately as much as VIX differs from SPX IV.
it is the 30 days IV of VIX. You get the next VIX options with expos T1, T2 bracketing 30 days. For each expo T you set a forward level Ft to the price of the matching VIX fut (discounting rfr)
now you build a strip, like... K0 is the strike lower/equal Ft closest to Ft
for each strike Ki you take mid quote OTM options (puts for Ki < K0, calls for Ki > K0; and at K0 using the avg of call and put.
now you use strike spacing deltaKi = (K(i+1) - K(i-1))/2, and you drop the dead wings (two consecutive 0-bid quotes,
now you can compute the risk neutral 30-day variance for each expiry using the standard VIX integral formula, 
linearly interpolate the variance between T1 and T2 to a target of 30 calendar days, and as last step you annualize the sqrt

for VIX ATM, you can use Black-76 model (with the relevant VIX futures as underlying)
you pick the tenor, get the forward (VIX options settle to the VIX future, not spot VIX!!!), set the ATM strike close to 50d,matching VIX fut (same expo), then take the mid of the VIX call and put at this ATM and solve the Black76 backwards.

comment.body:
Thanks for being open to questions! What period of time do you prefer for tracking 3M vol? 63 days? 

For forecasting distribution/risk levels across assets on any given day, do you recommend any smoothing over a recent look back period to calculate the expected RV for the next trading day? 

Do you have a preferred method on how you backtest strategies? Data source, libraries to use, etc?
comment.raw_body:
Thanks for being open to questions! What period of time do you prefer for tracking 3M vol? 63 days? 

For forecasting distribution/risk levels across assets on any given day, do you recommend any smoothing over a recent look back period to calculate the expected RV for the next trading day? 

Do you have a preferred method on how you backtest strategies? Data source, libraries to use, etc?

comment.body:
60-63 days can work yes. 

build a clea  risk neutral variance, either taking VIX or replicate it yourself from SPXW options. variance risk premium = (risk-neutral var – expected realized var)
implied var gives you extra short-run information, especially 1–20 trading days.
try HAR-RV, smooth microstructs by bid/ask mid filetring, drop short strikes and cap wings through SVI or cubic-spline before integral... 1-3 days ewma on RN variance to damp single print distrosions on daily tenors.. 
HAR on 1,5,22 days realized-kerned RV plus a jump proxy (RV - medRV) and take data releases risk events into accounts. estimate adaptive weight through rolling regression, refit monthly. 
beta/beta+gamma clipped to [0, 1], but in event heavy regimes the adaptive weight rises, in calm regimes it falls. (see Mincer–Zarnowitz for forecast combination)
then take positioning and cross asset structure, but thats another story...

check this https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/highfrequency/highfrequency.pdf
https://quantlib-python-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/termstructures/volatility.html
https://arch.readthedocs.io/en/stable/univariate/generated/arch.univariate.HARX.html
comment.raw_body:

implied var gives you extra short-run information, especially 1–20 trading days.
try HAR-RV, smooth microstructs by bid/ask mid filetring, drop short strikes and cap wings through SVI or cubic-spline before integral... 1-3 days ewma on RN variance to damp single print distrosions on daily tenors.. 
HAR on 1,5,22 days realized-kerned RV plus a jump proxy (RV - medRV) and take data releases risk events into accounts. estimate adaptive weight through rolling regression, refit monthly. 
beta/beta+gamma clipped to [0, 1], but in event heavy regimes the adaptive weight rises, in calm regimes it falls. (see Mincer–Zarnowitz for forecast combination)
then take positioning and cross asset structure, but thats another story...

check this https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/highfrequency/highfrequency.pdf

comment.body:
thats not what i personally use tbh, i use my own datasets. i will make my education series. I work on one more trading education post, and then i start the greeks education, and i will cover optionsdepth too.
to be quick: for grasp the sentiment, use the different timelapses, dates to see how the positioning evolved. not simply that "oh more puts came at this strike", but how the speed profile evolved.
you can make a very-very-very rough approximation of speed by taking the gamma from in the 1-3 delta strikes interval, and then derivate the curve. now you have values for each strikes, and take the slopes of different clusters (like 25 delta vs 5 delta, ATM versus 22 delta) etc. it is a good-enough proxy sometimes.
and check how it historically evolved. you will see where speed went longer and shorter... thats what matters.
 
vomma doesn't have a direct intraday influence, it has a weekly/monthly influence on volatility, reflexively effecting spot movements and premiums. you need teh 12 delta contracts for that, it is difficult to get

thats not what i personally use tbh, i use my own datasets. i will make my education series. I work on one more trading education post, and then i start the greeks education, and i will cover optionsdepth too.
to be quick: for grasp the sentiment, use the different timelapses, dates to see how the positioning evolved. not simply that "oh more puts came at this strike", but how the speed profile evolved.
you can make a very-very-very rough approximation of speed by taking the gamma from in the 1-3 delta strikes interval, and then derivate the curve. now you have values for each strikes, and take the slopes of different clusters (like 25 delta vs 5 delta, ATM versus 22 delta) etc. it is a good-enough proxy sometimes.
and check how it historically evolved. you will see where speed went longer and shorter... thats what matters.
 
vomma doesn't have a direct intraday influence, it has a weekly/monthly influence on volatility, reflexively effecting spot movements and premiums. you need teh 12 delta contracts for that, it is difficult to get

comment.body:
it is mostly creativity.
algos are only to get datas, but for macro you have to think about the narrative, what is the story the numbers are telling. algos cannot decipher that, they are not meant to, they are not thinking entities. just machines. 
for macro it is mostly valuation metrics, performances vs valuations, geopolitics flow, problems that the world must solve, potential challenges for sectors etc. 
read Graham's book, 
metrics don't work on the long run... i don't like to use these kinds of models, probabilistic predictions on more than 3 months long period.
6 months at very-very max. but one quartel is what feels "real"... so yepp, don't rely too much on numbers.
on the long run it is valuation
